context.pr_title: fix: no-play argument for API client
context.pr_body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file constructs and runs a Gradio web UI for Fish Speech (data preprocessing, training, inference, tensorboard, and model utilities). It also provides helper functions for file operations, process management (labeler, inference server, tensorboard), YAML handling, and subprocess command assembly for other tools in the repo.

### Architecture
- Top-level configuration and environment: sets PYTHON executable path, working directory, env overrides, loads Seafoam theme and i18n, loads YAML templates and static JS/CSS.
- Utility helpers: HTML builders, file I/O helpers, a filesystem-tree generator, audio conversion, YAML read/write, and process-killing utilities.
- Process management: functions that launch/kill subprocesses for labeler (change_label), inference server (change_infer), and tensorboard (tensorboard_process). They use subprocess.Popen/subprocess.run and global process handles p_label, p_infer, p_tensorboard.
- Model/tool listing and helpers: functions to list available checkpoints and build command lists for training, merging LoRA, quantization.
- Gradio UI: constructs the entire interactive UI using gr.Blocks with controls and event bindings; hooks widgets to the functions above. Finally calls demo.launch(inbrowser=True).

Components interact as follows:
- UI widgets call the helper/process functions (via click/change handlers) to perform filesystem operations, spawn subprocesses, and run training or utility scripts.
- Several functions build subprocess command-lines (using the global PYTHON) and set environment variables (notably GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT for inference).

### Functions & Classes
- PYTHON — top-level variable constructed as os.path.join(os.environ.get("PYTHON_FOLDERPATH", ""), "python"); used as the Python executable when launching subprocesses.
- seafoam — instance of Seafoam imported from fish_speech.webui.launch_utils used as the Gradio theme.
- build_html_error_message — returns an HTML string wrapping an escaped error message in red/bold; used to present error messages in the Gradio UI.
- build_html_ok_message — returns an HTML string wrapping an escaped success message in green/bold; used to present success messages in the Gradio UI.
- build_html_href — returns an HTML span with a link and message; used to provide actionable links in UI responses (e.g., open remote labeler).
- load_data_in_raw — reads and returns the contents of a file from a path as a string. Used to load static JS/CSS/HTML assets (animate.js, style.css, footer.html).
- kill_proc_tree — given a pid, attempts to terminate the process and all children using psutil and os.kill (SIGTERM). Used by kill_process to cleanly shut down processes on non-Windows systems.
- kill_process — platform-aware process-killing wrapper (uses taskkill on Windows or kill_proc_tree on other systems). Important for stopping subprocesses started by this script (labeler, infer, tensorboard).
- change_label — generator function that starts or stops a local labeler process (p_label). When starting, attempts to run a platform-specific label executable and yields an HTML message with a link to an online version. When stopping, kills p_label and yields a message. Bound to the "Open Labeler WebUI" checkbox.
- clean_infer_cache — deletes Gradio temp cache directory (temp/gradio) to clear cached audios, logging results or handling permission/file-not-found exceptions. Called prior to launching the inference server.
- change_infer — generator function that starts or stops the inference server subprocess stored in p_infer. When starting, sets GRADIO_SERVER_NAME/GRADIO_SERVER_PORT in env, yields success message with URL, calls clean_infer_cache, then launches a subprocess:
  - Command assembled: [PYTHON, "tools/run_webui.py", "--decoder-checkpoint-path", infer_decoder_model, "--decoder-config-name", infer_decoder_config, "--llama-checkpoint-path", infer_llama_model] + (["--compile"] if infer_compile == "Yes" else [])
  - Uses env with GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT set to the chosen host/port.
  When stopping, kills the process and yields a closure message.
  This is directly relevant to how the inference server is launched and which CLI flags are sent to the tool.
- generate_tree — recursively builds an HTML-like string representing directory/file tree entries with specified max_depth; used to show folder structure in UI.
- new_explorer — returns a gr.Markdown component displaying generate_tree output for a path; used to populate the file explorer widget.
- add_item — handles adding a source folder/file entry into internal items/dict_items used as input sources. Validates folders/files, copies/moves later, and returns updated gr.Checkboxgroup and status HTML message.
- remove_items — removes selected items from the internal items/dict_items stores, returns updated Checkboxgroup and status HTML message.
- show_selected — simple presenter returning selected options joined into a display string.
- convert_to_mono_in_place — loads audio via pydub AudioSegment and converts stereo/multi-channel audio file to mono, rewriting the same path; used during file preprocessing to ensure single-channel audio.
- list_copy — given a filelist (list_file_path) iterates lines of "wav_path|speaker_name|language|text", copies or moves audio and corresponding .lab files into the data preprocessing directory (data_pre_output), converts audio to mono, and returns an OK HTML message. If method == "Move", it rewrites the filelist to reflect new locations.
- check_files — main preprocessing handler that iterates dict_items and:
  - For folder items: copies or moves the folder into the given data_path, converts audio files (wav/flac/mp3) to mono, optionally runs transcription using tools/whisper_asr.py by assembling a transcribe_cmd list:
    [PYTHON, "tools/whisper_asr.py", "--model-size", label_model, "--device", label_device, "--audio-dir", tar_path, "--save-dir", tar_path, "--language", cur_lang] (+ ["--initial-prompt", initial_prompt] if provided)
  - For file items: calls list_copy.
  Returns an OK HTML message and updates the file explorer display. Important: constructs the CLI call for whisper_asr.py.
- generate_folder_name — returns timestamp string (YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS) for naming outputs/temporary folders.
- train_process — orchestrates training flows for VQGAN or LLAMA. For LLAMA it:
  - Computes backend ("nccl" for linux else "gloo").
  - Calls tools to extract VQGAN features and to build dataset:
    - [PYTHON, "tools/vqgan/extract_vq.py", str(data_pre_output), ...]
    - [PYTHON, "tools/llama/build_dataset.py", "--input", str(data_pre_output), ...]
  - Prepares project names and constructs a long command list train_cmd to invoke fish_speech/train.py with --config-name "text2semantic_finetune" and many Hydra-style overrides (trainer.strategy.process_group_backend, train_dataset.proto_files, model.optimizer.lr, trainer.max_steps, etc.). It may add a LoRA config override if llama_use_lora is True. Then runs subprocess.run(train_cmd).
  Returns an OK HTML message when done. Important: constructs a complex CLI command for training.
- tensorboard_process — generator function to start/stop TensorBoard launched as a subprocess (p_tensorboard). When starting, determines prefix (native tensorboard or fishenv wrapped), yields an OK message with URL, runs tensorboard with flags:
  prefix + ["--logdir", tensorboard_dir, "--host", host, "--port", port, "--reload_interval", "120"]
  When stopping, kills the process and yields a closing message. Bound to a checkbox in UI.
- fresh_tb_dir — returns gr.Dropdown with choices of "results/**/tensorboard/" directories — used to refresh tensorboard path dropdown choices.
- list_decoder_models — returns checkpoint paths matching "checkpoints/**/firefly*.pth". Used to populate the decoder model dropdown for inference.
- list_llama_models — returns a list of directories containing LLaMA model checkpoints from multiple glob patterns, sorted. Used to populate LLaMA model dropdowns.
- list_lora_llama_models — returns list of LoRA ckpt files under results; used for selecting LoRA checkpoints.
- fresh_decoder_model — returns gr.Dropdown(choices=list_decoder_models()). UI helper to refresh decoder model choices.
- fresh_llama_ckpt — returns gr.Dropdown showing choices [i18n("latest"), i18n("new")] + project dirs or lora dirs depending on llama_use_lora flag. Used to refresh the LLaMA checkpoint selector.
- fresh_llama_model — returns gr.Dropdown(choices=list_llama_models()). UI helper.
- llama_lora_merge — checks existence of lora_weight and llama_weight paths, shows a warning, constructs merge_cmd:
  [PYTHON, "tools/llama/merge_lora.py", "--lora-config", "r_8_alpha_16", "--lora-weight", lora_weight, "--output", llama_lora_output + "_" + generate_folder_name()]
  Runs subprocess.run(merge_cmd) and returns OK message. Used in Merge LoRA UI tab.
- llama_quantify — validates llama_weight path, shows warning, builds quantify_cmd:
  [PYTHON, "tools/llama/quantize.py", "--checkpoint-path", llama_weight, "--mode", quantify_mode, "--timestamp", now]
  Runs subprocess.run(quantify_cmd), computes resulting quantized path and returns OK message containing the quantize path. Used in Model Quantization tab.
- load_yaml_data_in_fact — reads YAML from a given path and returns parsed YAML (safe_load). Used to load default configs vqgan_yml_path and llama_yml_path.
- write_yaml_data_in_fact — writes YAML data to a given path using yaml.safe_dump; used as a helper to persist YAML edits (not heavily used in the file).

- (UI binding block) The bottom section creates a Gradio Blocks demo with many widgets and binds them to the functions above via .click/.change/.select handlers. Key event bindings relevant to process starts:
  - add_button.click -> add_item
  - remove_button.click -> remove_items
  - if_label.change -> change_label
  - train_btn.click -> train_process
  - if_tb.change -> tensorboard_process
  - admit_btn.click -> check_files
  - infer_checkbox.change -> change_infer
  - many dropdowns wired to fresh_* functions to refresh available choices

- demo.launch(inbrowser=True) — launches the Gradio web UI in a browser (no argparse used here).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
Agent asked to find CLI argument definitions for a --play option and API client flags, and to search for argparse usage, '--play' flag, or BooleanOptionalAction; and functions that parse arguments and start API client/server.

Findings in this file:

- This file does start external tools (inference server, whisper ASR, training scripts, tensorboard) by assembling command-line argument lists passed to subprocess.run or subprocess.Popen. Relevant commands and flags used in this file include:
  - change_infer launches tools/run_webui.py with flags:
    --decoder-checkpoint-path <infer_decoder_model>
    --decoder-config-name <infer_decoder_config>
    --llama-checkpoint-path <infer_llama_model>
    optionally: --compile
    Also sets GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT environment variables for the launched process.
  - check_files assembles whisper ASR call:
    [PYTHON, "tools/whisper_asr.py", "--model-size", label_model, "--device", label_device, "--audio-dir", tar_path, "--save-dir", tar_path, "--language", cur_lang] (+ ["--initial-prompt", initial_prompt] if provided)
  - train_process assembles a long train_cmd to run fish_speech/train.py with --config-name text2semantic_finetune and many hydra-style overrides (trainer.*, data.*, model.optimizer.lr, etc.). It uses PYTHON as the executable.
  - tensorboard_process launches the tensorboard executable with flags: --logdir, --host, --port, --reload_interval.
  - llama_lora_merge and llama_quantify launch tools/llama/merge_lora.py and tools/llama/quantize.py with their own flags.
- If you are searching specifically for a CLI --play option or API client flags, this file does not define them. If such flags exist, they would likely be defined in the scripts that this file invokes (e.g., tools/run_webui.py, fish_speech/train.py, tools/whisper_asr.py, tools/llama/*). The current file only constructs calls to those scripts and supplies certain flags (see above). The closest relevant items are the subprocess argument lists shown for run_webui.py and whisper_asr.py and the environment variables set (GRADIO_SERVER_NAME/GRADIO_SERVER_PORT).

summarization.summary:
### File Overview
This file constructs and runs a Gradio web UI for Fish Speech (data preprocessing, training, inference, tensorboard, and model utilities). It also provides helper functions for file operations, process management (labeler, inference server, tensorboard), YAML handling, and subprocess command assembly for other tools in the repo.

### Architecture
- Top-level configuration and environment: sets PYTHON executable path, working directory, env overrides, loads Seafoam theme and i18n, loads YAML templates and static JS/CSS.
- Utility helpers: HTML builders, file I/O helpers, a filesystem-tree generator, audio conversion, YAML read/write, and process-killing utilities.
- Process management: functions that launch/kill subprocesses for labeler (change_label), inference server (change_infer), and tensorboard (tensorboard_process). They use subprocess.Popen/subprocess.run and global process handles p_label, p_infer, p_tensorboard.
- Model/tool listing and helpers: functions to list available checkpoints and build command lists for training, merging LoRA, quantization.
- Gradio UI: constructs the entire interactive UI using gr.Blocks with controls and event bindings; hooks widgets to the functions above. Finally calls demo.launch(inbrowser=True).

Components interact as follows:
- UI widgets call the helper/process functions (via click/change handlers) to perform filesystem operations, spawn subprocesses, and run training or utility scripts.
- Several functions build subprocess command-lines (using the global PYTHON) and set environment variables (notably GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT for inference).

### Functions & Classes
- PYTHON — top-level variable constructed as os.path.join(os.environ.get("PYTHON_FOLDERPATH", ""), "python"); used as the Python executable when launching subprocesses.
- seafoam — instance of Seafoam imported from fish_speech.webui.launch_utils used as the Gradio theme.
- build_html_error_message — returns an HTML string wrapping an escaped error message in red/bold; used to present error messages in the Gradio UI.
- build_html_ok_message — returns an HTML string wrapping an escaped success message in green/bold; used to present success messages in the Gradio UI.
- build_html_href — returns an HTML span with a link and message; used to provide actionable links in UI responses (e.g., open remote labeler).
- load_data_in_raw — reads and returns the contents of a file from a path as a string. Used to load static JS/CSS/HTML assets (animate.js, style.css, footer.html).
- kill_proc_tree — given a pid, attempts to terminate the process and all children using psutil and os.kill (SIGTERM). Used by kill_process to cleanly shut down processes on non-Windows systems.
- kill_process — platform-aware process-killing wrapper (uses taskkill on Windows or kill_proc_tree on other systems). Important for stopping subprocesses started by this script (labeler, infer, tensorboard).
- change_label — generator function that starts or stops a local labeler process (p_label). When starting, attempts to run a platform-specific label executable and yields an HTML message with a link to an online version. When stopping, kills p_label and yields a message. Bound to the "Open Labeler WebUI" checkbox.
- clean_infer_cache — deletes Gradio temp cache directory (temp/gradio) to clear cached audios, logging results or handling permission/file-not-found exceptions. Called prior to launching the inference server.
- change_infer — generator function that starts or stops the inference server subprocess stored in p_infer. When starting, sets GRADIO_SERVER_NAME/GRADIO_SERVER_PORT in env, yields success message with URL, calls clean_infer_cache, then launches a subprocess:
  - Command assembled: [PYTHON, "tools/run_webui.py", "--decoder-checkpoint-path", infer_decoder_model, "--decoder-config-name", infer_decoder_config, "--llama-checkpoint-path", infer_llama_model] + (["--compile"] if infer_compile == "Yes" else [])
  - Uses env with GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT set to the chosen host/port.
  When stopping, kills the process and yields a closure message.
  This is directly relevant to how the inference server is launched and which CLI flags are sent to the tool.
- generate_tree — recursively builds an HTML-like string representing directory/file tree entries with specified max_depth; used to show folder structure in UI.
- new_explorer — returns a gr.Markdown component displaying generate_tree output for a path; used to populate the file explorer widget.
- add_item — handles adding a source folder/file entry into internal items/dict_items used as input sources. Validates folders/files, copies/moves later, and returns updated gr.Checkboxgroup and status HTML message.
- remove_items — removes selected items from the internal items/dict_items stores, returns updated Checkboxgroup and status HTML message.
- show_selected — simple presenter returning selected options joined into a display string.
- convert_to_mono_in_place — loads audio via pydub AudioSegment and converts stereo/multi-channel audio file to mono, rewriting the same path; used during file preprocessing to ensure single-channel audio.
- list_copy — given a filelist (list_file_path) iterates lines of "wav_path|speaker_name|language|text", copies or moves audio and corresponding .lab files into the data preprocessing directory (data_pre_output), converts audio to mono, and returns an OK HTML message. If method == "Move", it rewrites the filelist to reflect new locations.
- check_files — main preprocessing handler that iterates dict_items and:
  - For folder items: copies or moves the folder into the given data_path, converts audio files (wav/flac/mp3) to mono, optionally runs transcription using tools/whisper_asr.py by assembling a transcribe_cmd list:
    [PYTHON, "tools/whisper_asr.py", "--model-size", label_model, "--device", label_device, "--audio-dir", tar_path, "--save-dir", tar_path, "--language", cur_lang] (+ ["--initial-prompt", initial_prompt] if provided)
  - For file items: calls list_copy.
  Returns an OK HTML message and updates the file explorer display. Important: constructs the CLI call for whisper_asr.py.
- generate_folder_name — returns timestamp string (YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS) for naming outputs/temporary folders.
- train_process — orchestrates training flows for VQGAN or LLAMA. For LLAMA it:
  - Computes backend ("nccl" for linux else "gloo").
  - Calls tools to extract VQGAN features and to build dataset:
    - [PYTHON, "tools/vqgan/extract_vq.py", str(data_pre_output), ...]
    - [PYTHON, "tools/llama/build_dataset.py", "--input", str(data_pre_output), ...]
  - Prepares project names and constructs a long command list train_cmd to invoke fish_speech/train.py with --config-name "text2semantic_finetune" and many Hydra-style overrides (trainer.strategy.process_group_backend, train_dataset.proto_files, model.optimizer.lr, trainer.max_steps, etc.). It may add a LoRA config override if llama_use_lora is True. Then runs subprocess.run(train_cmd).
  Returns an OK HTML message when done. Important: constructs a complex CLI command for training.
- tensorboard_process — generator function to start/stop TensorBoard launched as a subprocess (p_tensorboard). When starting, determines prefix (native tensorboard or fishenv wrapped), yields an OK message with URL, runs tensorboard with flags:
  prefix + ["--logdir", tensorboard_dir, "--host", host, "--port", port, "--reload_interval", "120"]
  When stopping, kills the process and yields a closing message. Bound to a checkbox in UI.
- fresh_tb_dir — returns gr.Dropdown with choices of "results/**/tensorboard/" directories — used to refresh tensorboard path dropdown choices.
- list_decoder_models — returns checkpoint paths matching "checkpoints/**/firefly*.pth". Used to populate the decoder model dropdown for inference.
- list_llama_models — returns a list of directories containing LLaMA model checkpoints from multiple glob patterns, sorted. Used to populate LLaMA model dropdowns.
- list_lora_llama_models — returns list of LoRA ckpt files under results; used for selecting LoRA checkpoints.
- fresh_decoder_model — returns gr.Dropdown(choices=list_decoder_models()). UI helper to refresh decoder model choices.
- fresh_llama_ckpt — returns gr.Dropdown showing choices [i18n("latest"), i18n("new")] + project dirs or lora dirs depending on llama_use_lora flag. Used to refresh the LLaMA checkpoint selector.
- fresh_llama_model — returns gr.Dropdown(choices=list_llama_models()). UI helper.
- llama_lora_merge — checks existence of lora_weight and llama_weight paths, shows a warning, constructs merge_cmd:
  [PYTHON, "tools/llama/merge_lora.py", "--lora-config", "r_8_alpha_16", "--lora-weight", lora_weight, "--output", llama_lora_output + "_" + generate_folder_name()]
  Runs subprocess.run(merge_cmd) and returns OK message. Used in Merge LoRA UI tab.
- llama_quantify — validates llama_weight path, shows warning, builds quantify_cmd:
  [PYTHON, "tools/llama/quantize.py", "--checkpoint-path", llama_weight, "--mode", quantify_mode, "--timestamp", now]
  Runs subprocess.run(quantify_cmd), computes resulting quantized path and returns OK message containing the quantize path. Used in Model Quantization tab.
- load_yaml_data_in_fact — reads YAML from a given path and returns parsed YAML (safe_load). Used to load default configs vqgan_yml_path and llama_yml_path.
- write_yaml_data_in_fact — writes YAML data to a given path using yaml.safe_dump; used as a helper to persist YAML edits (not heavily used in the file).

- (UI binding block) The bottom section creates a Gradio Blocks demo with many widgets and binds them to the functions above via .click/.change/.select handlers. Key event bindings relevant to process starts:
  - add_button.click -> add_item
  - remove_button.click -> remove_items
  - if_label.change -> change_label
  - train_btn.click -> train_process
  - if_tb.change -> tensorboard_process
  - admit_btn.click -> check_files
  - infer_checkbox.change -> change_infer
  - many dropdowns wired to fresh_* functions to refresh available choices

- demo.launch(inbrowser=True) — launches the Gradio web UI in a browser (no argparse used here).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal

    --decoder-checkpoint-path <infer_decoder_model>

    optionally: --compile
    Also sets GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT environment variables for the launched process.

import argparse
import base64
import wave

import ormsgpack
import pyaudio
import requests
from pydub import AudioSegment
from pydub.playback import play

from tools.file import audio_to_bytes, read_ref_text
from tools.schema import ServeReferenceAudio, ServeTTSRequest


def parse_args():

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Send a WAV file and text to a server and receive synthesized audio.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        "-u",
        type=str,
        default="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/tts",
        help="URL of the server",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--text", "-t", type=str, required=True, help="Text to be synthesized"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_id",
        "-id",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())

### File Overview
This file implements a simple CLI client that packages text and optional reference audios/texts into a ServeTTSRequest, sends it to a TTS server (POSTing msgpack), and either streams playback via pyaudio or saves (and optionally plays) the returned audio file.

### Architecture
- parse_args() builds an argparse.ArgumentParser and defines all CLI options, returning parsed args.
- The main runtime (guarded by if __name__ == "__main__") uses parse_args(), prepares reference audio/text payloads (using tools.file functions), constructs a dictionary that is validated into a ServeTTSRequest (from tools.schema), packs that with ormsgpack, and POSTs to args.url via requests.
- Two response modes: streaming (args.streaming True) uses pyaudio and wave to play/write chunks from response.iter_content; non-streaming writes the whole response.content to a file and uses pydub.AudioSegment and pydub.playback.play to play audio if requested.
- External components used:

import argparse
import base64
import wave

import ormsgpack
import pyaudio
import requests
from pydub import AudioSegment
from pydub.playback import play

from tools.file import audio_to_bytes, read_ref_text
from tools.schema import ServeReferenceAudio, ServeTTSRequest


def parse_args():

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Send a WAV file and text to a server and receive synthesized audio.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        "-u",
        type=str,
        default="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/tts",
        help="URL of the server",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--text", "-t", type=str, required=True, help="Text to be synthesized"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_id",
        "-id",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())

import os
from argparse import ArgumentParser
from pathlib import Path

import pyrootutils
import torch
from loguru import logger

pyrootutils.setup_root(__file__, indicator=".project-root", pythonpath=True)

from tools.inference_engine import TTSInferenceEngine
from tools.llama.generate import launch_thread_safe_queue
from tools.schema import ServeTTSRequest
from tools.vqgan.inference import load_model as load_decoder_model
from tools.webui import build_app
from tools.webui.inference import get_inference_wrapper

# Make einx happy
os.environ["EINX_FILTER_TRACEBACK"] = "false"


def parse_args():
    parser = ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument(
        "--llama-checkpoint-path",
        type=Path,
        default="checkpoints/fish-speech-1.5",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--decoder-checkpoint-path",
        type=Path,
        default="checkpoints/fish-speech-1.5/firefly-gan-vq-fsq-8x1024-21hz-generator.pth",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--decoder-config-name", type=str, default="firefly_gan_vq")
    parser.add_argument("--device", type=str, default="cuda")
    parser.add_argument("--half", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--compile", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--max-gradio-length", type=int, default=0)
    parser.add_argument("--theme", type=str, default="light")

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    args = parse_args()
    args.precision = torch.half if args.half else torch.bfloat16

    # Check if MPS or CUDA is available
    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
        args.device = "mps"
        logger.info("mps is available, running on mps.")
    elif not torch.cuda.is_available():
        logger.info("CUDA is not available, running on CPU.")
        args.device = "cpu"

    logger.info("Loading Llama model...")
    llama_queue = launch_thread_safe_queue(
        checkpoint_path=args.llama_checkpoint_path,
        device=args.device,
        precision=args.precision,
        compile=args.compile,
    )

    logger.info("Loading VQ-GAN model...")
    decoder_model = load_decoder_model(
        config_name=args.decoder_config_name,
        checkpoint_path=args.decoder_checkpoint_path,
        device=args.device,
    )

    logger.info("Decoder model loaded, warming up...")

    # Create the inference engine
    inference_engine = TTSInferenceEngine(
        llama_queue=llama_queue,
        decoder_model=decoder_model,
        compile=args.compile,
        precision=args.precision,
    )

    # Dry run to check if the model is loaded correctly and avoid the first-time latency
    list(
        inference_engine.inference(
            ServeTTSRequest(
                text="Hello world.",
                references=[],
                reference_id=None,
                max_new_tokens=1024,
                chunk_length=200,
                top_p=0.7,
                repetition_penalty=1.5,
                temperature=0.7,
                format="wav",
            )
        )
    )

    logger.info("Warming up done, launching the web UI...")

    # Get the inference function with the immutable arguments
    inference_fct = get_inference_wrapper(inference_engine)

    app = build_app(inference_fct, args.theme)
    app.launch(show_api=True)

import os
from argparse import ArgumentParser
from pathlib import Path

import pyrootutils
import torch
from loguru import logger

pyrootutils.setup_root(__file__, indicator=".project-root", pythonpath=True)

from tools.inference_engine import TTSInferenceEngine
from tools.llama.generate import launch_thread_safe_queue
from tools.schema import ServeTTSRequest
from tools.vqgan.inference import load_model as load_decoder_model
from tools.webui import build_app
from tools.webui.inference import get_inference_wrapper

# Make einx happy
os.environ["EINX_FILTER_TRACEBACK"] = "false"


def parse_args():
    parser = ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument(
        "--llama-checkpoint-path",
        type=Path,
        default="checkpoints/fish-speech-1.5",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--decoder-checkpoint-path",
        type=Path,
        default="checkpoints/fish-speech-1.5/firefly-gan-vq-fsq-8x1024-21hz-generator.pth",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--decoder-config-name", type=str, default="firefly_gan_vq")
    parser.add_argument("--device", type=str, default="cuda")
    parser.add_argument("--half", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--compile", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--max-gradio-length", type=int, default=0)
    parser.add_argument("--theme", type=str, default="light")

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    args = parse_args()
    args.precision = torch.half if args.half else torch.bfloat16

    # Check if MPS or CUDA is available
    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
        args.device = "mps"
        logger.info("mps is available, running on mps.")
    elif not torch.cuda.is_available():
        logger.info("CUDA is not available, running on CPU.")
        args.device = "cpu"

    logger.info("Loading Llama model...")
    llama_queue = launch_thread_safe_queue(
        checkpoint_path=args.llama_checkpoint_path,
        device=args.device,
        precision=args.precision,
        compile=args.compile,
    )

    logger.info("Loading VQ-GAN model...")
    decoder_model = load_decoder_model(
        config_name=args.decoder_config_name,
        checkpoint_path=args.decoder_checkpoint_path,
        device=args.device,
    )

    logger.info("Decoder model loaded, warming up...")

    # Create the inference engine
    inference_engine = TTSInferenceEngine(
        llama_queue=llama_queue,
        decoder_model=decoder_model,
        compile=args.compile,
        precision=args.precision,
    )

    # Dry run to check if the model is loaded correctly and avoid the first-time latency
    list(
        inference_engine.inference(
            ServeTTSRequest(
                text="Hello world.",
                references=[],
                reference_id=None,
                max_new_tokens=1024,
                chunk_length=200,
                top_p=0.7,
                repetition_penalty=1.5,
                temperature=0.7,
                format="wav",
            )
        )
    )

    logger.info("Warming up done, launching the web UI...")

    # Get the inference function with the immutable arguments
    inference_fct = get_inference_wrapper(inference_engine)

    app = build_app(inference_fct, args.theme)
    app.launch(show_api=True)

from threading import Lock

import pyrootutils
import uvicorn
from kui.asgi import FactoryClass, HTTPException, HttpRoute, Kui, OpenAPI, Routes
from loguru import logger

pyrootutils.setup_root(__file__, indicator=".project-root", pythonpath=True)

from tools.server.api_utils import MsgPackRequest, parse_args
from tools.server.exception_handler import ExceptionHandler
from tools.server.model_manager import ModelManager
from tools.server.views import (
    ASRView,
    ChatView,
    HealthView,
    TTSView,
    VQGANDecodeView,
    VQGANEncodeView,
)


class API(ExceptionHandler):
    def __init__(self):
        self.args = parse_args()
        self.routes = [
            ("/v1/health", HealthView),
            ("/v1/vqgan/encode", VQGANEncodeView),
            ("/v1/vqgan/decode", VQGANDecodeView),
            ("/v1/asr", ASRView),
            ("/v1/tts", TTSView),
            ("/v1/chat", ChatView),
        ]
        self.routes = Routes([HttpRoute(path, view) for path, view in self.routes])

        self.openapi = OpenAPI(
            {
                "title": "Fish Speech API",
                "version": "1.5.0",
            },
        ).routes

        # Initialize the app
        self.app = Kui(
            routes=self.routes + self.openapi[1:],  # Remove the default route
            exception_handlers={
                HTTPException: self.http_exception_handler,
                Exception: self.other_exception_handler,
            },
            factory_class=FactoryClass(http=MsgPackRequest),
            cors_config={},
        )

        # Add the state variables
        self.app.state.lock = Lock()
        self.app.state.device = self.args.device
        self.app.state.max_text_length = self.args.max_text_length

        # Associate the app with the model manager
        self.app.on_startup(self.initialize_app)

    async def initialize_app(self, app: Kui):
        # Make the ModelManager available to the views
        app.state.model_manager = ModelManager(
            mode=self.args.mode,
            device=self.args.device,
            half=self.args.half,
            compile=self.args.compile,
            asr_enabled=self.args.load_asr_model,
            llama_checkpoint_path=self.args.llama_checkpoint_path,
            decoder_checkpoint_path=self.args.decoder_checkpoint_path,
            decoder_config_name=self.args.decoder_config_name,
        )

        logger.info(f"Startup done, listening server at http://{self.args.listen}")


# Each worker process created by Uvicorn has its own memory space,
# meaning that models and variables are not shared between processes.
# Therefore, any variables (like `llama_queue` or `decoder_model`)
# will not be shared across workers.

# Multi-threading for deep learning can cause issues, such as inconsistent
# outputs if multiple threads access the same buffers simultaneously.
# Instead, it's better to use multiprocessing or independent models per thread.

if __name__ == "__main__":

    api = API()
    host, port = api.args.listen.split(":")

    uvicorn.run(
        api.app,
        host=host,
        port=int(port),
        workers=api.args.workers,
        log_level="info",
    )

import argparse
import base64
import wave

import ormsgpack
import pyaudio
import requests
from pydub import AudioSegment
from pydub.playback import play

from tools.file import audio_to_bytes, read_ref_text
from tools.schema import ServeReferenceAudio, ServeTTSRequest


def parse_args():

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Send a WAV file and text to a server and receive synthesized audio.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        "-u",
        type=str,
        default="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/tts",
        help="URL of the server",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--text", "-t", type=str, required=True, help="Text to be synthesized"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_id",
        "-id",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())

import argparse
import base64
import wave

import ormsgpack
import pyaudio
import requests
from pydub import AudioSegment
from pydub.playback import play

from tools.file import audio_to_bytes, read_ref_text
from tools.schema import ServeReferenceAudio, ServeTTSRequest


def parse_args():

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Send a WAV file and text to a server and receive synthesized audio.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        "-u",
        type=str,
        default="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/tts",
        help="URL of the server",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--text", "-t", type=str, required=True, help="Text to be synthesized"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_id",
        "-id",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())

        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())

### File Overview
This script is a command-line API client that builds a ServeTTSRequest from local inputs (text and optional reference audios/texts), sends it to a TTS server via requests (using ORMsgPack serialization), and either streams playback via pyaudio or writes the returned audio to disk. The file defines parse_args() and contains the main runtime flow inside the if __name__ == "__main__": block.

### Architecture
- Top-level imports bring in argparse, audio handling (pydub, pyaudio, wave), HTTP and serialization (requests, ormsgpack), and helper objects/functions from tools.file and tools.schema.
- parse_args() (top-level function) constructs and returns an argparse.ArgumentParser result containing numerous CLI flags used to drive request creation and audio handling.
- The if __name__ == "__main__": block:
  - calls parse_args()
  - prepares reference audio/text inputs (using audio_to_bytes and read_ref_text)
  - constructs a data dict and validates it via ServeTTSRequest(**data)
  - posts to the server with ormsgpack.packb(..., option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC)
  - handles the response either as a streaming waveform written to a .wav file while playing it via pyaudio, or as a non-streamed file saved to disk and optionally played via pydub.playback.play

### Functions & Classes
- parse_args — top-level function that builds and returns the argparse.Namespace from parser.parse_args(). It defines all CLI flags used by the script, including:
  - "--url" / "-u"
  - "--text" / "-t" (required)

  - "--repetition_penalty"
  - "--temperature"

The agent asked to confirm the exact location and definition of parse_args — it is declared near the top of the file immediately after imports as def parse_args(): and returns parser.parse_args().

- audio_to_bytes — imported from tools.file. Role: convert a reference audio file path to bytes for inclusion into the request. Importance: used when args.reference_audio is provided to create byte_audios.

- read_ref_text — imported from tools.file. Role: read reference text from a file path. Importance: used when args.reference_text is provided to populate ref_texts for the request.

- pyaudio.PyAudio — used to create an audio output stream. Importance: enables real-time playback for streaming responses.

- pyaudio.paInt16 — used as the audio_format for the output stream (assumes 16-bit PCM). Importance: determines sample width and is used to set wave file sample width via p.get_sample_size(audio_format).

  - wf.setnchannels — set channels on the wave file.
  - wf.setsampwidth — set sample width (uses p.get_sample_size).
  - wf.setframerate — set sample rate.
  - wf.writeframesraw — write raw audio frames as they are received.

output.pr_title: fix: no-play argument for API client
output.pr_body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.
output.masca_optimized:
Motivation: Fix a CLI bug where `--play` in tools/api_client.py is declared with type=bool and default=True, which prevents passing `--no-play` or setting it to False via the CLI. The intended behavior is to let users set play to True/False explicitly (e.g., --play / --no-play or --no-play to disable playback).

Analysis: The relevant code is parse_args() in tools/api_client.py. It currently registers boolean-like flags using type=bool (not argparse actions). Specifically:
- parser.add_argument("--play", type=bool, default=True, ...)
- parser.add_argument("--streaming", type=bool, default=False, ...)
- parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)

Usage: args.play is checked in the non-streaming branch (if args.play: play(audio)), and args.streaming is used for request streaming and branching. Changing how --play is parsed to use argparse.BooleanOptionalAction preserves boolean semantics and adds a --no-play option (and `--no-streaming`/`--no-normalize` if desired).

Scope: Minimal change to tools/api_client.py. Replace the add_argument call for --play to use action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction (keep default True). Optionally change --streaming and --normalize similarly for consistency and correct CLI behavior.

Changes to make (high level):
- In parse_args() in tools/api_client.py, replace parser.add_argument(... type=bool ...) for --play with parser.add_argument('--play', action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True, help=...). Remove type=bool. No other code changes required, because args.play is still a boolean.
- (Optional but recommended) Do the same for --streaming and --normalize so they accept --no-streaming and --no-normalize.

Compatibility & Risks: Low risk. This keeps default behavior (default True/False values remain) but adds explicit negation flags. If the runtime environment uses an older Python where BooleanOptionalAction is unavailable, that would be an issue — ensure supported minimum Python version. No changes to how args.play is consumed are required.

You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```

Ensure Python runtime supports BooleanOptionalAction; otherwise use store_true/store_false pairs as a fallback.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Replace boolean flag parsing with argparse.BooleanOptionalAction for --play (and optionally --streaming and --normalize)
steps.reason: The current parser defines boolean flags using type=bool which does not produce a proper negatable CLI flag (e.g., --no-play). Using argparse.BooleanOptionalAction for --play (and optionally for --streaming and --normalize) makes the CLI accept both --play and --no-play, preserves the current defaults, and leaves args.play as a boolean for the rest of the code.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: Adds new negation flags (--no-play, and --no-streaming / --no-normalize if changed), but keeps defaults the same so existing invocations continue to work. Tests: update or add CLI unit/integration tests to verify --no-play sets args.play to False, and that default behavior remains unchanged. Dependents: any scripts or tooling that previously assumed the old non-negatable behavior should continue to work, but code that relied on passing explicit boolean-like values via the CLI (e.g., --play False) will need to use the new negation flag instead. Compatibility: argparse.BooleanOptionalAction was introduced in Python 3.9 — if the project supports older Python versions, either guard the change with a version check or implement a fallback using store_true/store_false pairs (e.g., --play / --no-play manually). API: no runtime API changes beyond CLI flags; args.play remains a boolean, so downstream code using args.play, args.streaming, args.normalize remains unchanged.

step_plan.summary: Fix the CLI boolean parsing in tools/api_client.py by using argparse.BooleanOptionalAction for --play so users can pass --no-play to disable playback; optionally apply the same fix to --streaming and --normalize for consistent negation flags.
